rag_rules: Fix unresolved property associations like 'Source_Data_Size' by removing invalid clauses or ensuring AADL references are valid in scope.
Avoid generating AGREE clauses that reference AADL timing properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
generic_rules: Only reference AADL identifiers that are visible in the current scope; do not introduce package-qualified names not present in the model.
Ensure AGREE expressions use only declared types (int, real, bool) and avoid invalid scalar type references.
Validate that guarantees and assumptions refer to valid component features or ports in their respective scopes.
Remove clauses that reference opaque Boolean or numeric data types unless the requirement and AADL model support scalar representations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid annex scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
AGREE constants and equations must declare valid scalar types (int, real, bool) and reference only visible identifiers.
anti_patterns: Do not generate AGREE expressions that assume AADL property associations are automatically available as variables.
Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-level paths in component type annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about missing property associations
Component type annexes for implementation-level bindings
Implementation-level guarantees in component type scopes
